name: FMT attenuated old mouse frailty through gut-muscle-axis markers
summary: FMT improved old-mouse frailty and muscle-related readouts with gut barrier, inflammation, SCFA, and LPS/TLR4 pathway support.
statement: In old mice, FMT improved frailty, grip strength, low physical activity, running speed, muscle mass, intestinal ecological imbalance, barrier function, systemic inflammation, SCFA-producing bacteria, propionic acid, LPS, and TLR4/NF-kB-linked inflammatory signaling.
population_or_model: Young and old mice receiving bidirectional FMT
time_horizon: Post-FMT frailty, muscle, inflammation, barrier, and metabolite assessment
quantitative_note: The abstract reports grip strength p=0.036, low physical activity p=0.020, running speed p=0.048, frailty score p=0.027, serum TNF-alpha p=0.002, IL-6 p<0.001, propionic acid p=0.028, and LPS p<0.001.
caveats: The result is mouse frailty biology, not a human frailty intervention.
FMT composition, donor age, dosing, and pretreatment details need review before comparing with human capsule trials.
Mechanistic attribution to SCFAs and LPS/TLR4 signaling remains preclinical.
